title: धन्वन्तरि मन्त्र
devanagariText:
॥ श्री धन्वन्तरि मन्त्र ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
शंखं चक्रं जलौकां च दधदमृतभाजनम्।
चतुर्भुजं सुप्रसन्नं ध्यायेद्धन्वन्तरिं हरिम्॥

नीलोत्पलदलश्यामं पद्मालङ्कृतभूषणम्।
चतुर्भुजं हरिं वन्दे धन्वन्तरिमनामयम्॥

॥ धन्वन्तरि मूल मन्त्र ॥
ॐ नमो भगवते वासुदेवाय धन्वन्तरये।
अमृतकलशहस्ताय सर्वामयविनाशनाय
त्रैलोक्यनाथाय श्रीमहाविष्णवे नमः॥१॥

॥ धन्वन्तरि गायत्री ॥
ॐ धन्वन्तरये विद्महे दिवोदासाय धीमहि।
तन्नो विष्णुः प्रचोदयात्॥२॥

॥ धन्वन्तरि बीज मन्त्र ॥
ॐ ह्रीं धन्वन्तरये नमः॥३॥

॥ आरोग्य मन्त्र ॥
ॐ ऐं ह्रीं श्रीं क्लीं धन्वन्तरये नमः।
सर्वरोगनिवारणाय सर्वव्याधिविनाशनाय
महाविष्णुस्वरूपाय धन्वन्तरये नमो नमः॥४॥

॥ रोगनाशन स्तुति ॥
नमामि धन्वन्तरिमादिदेवं सुरासुरैर्वन्दितपादपद्मम्।
लोके जरारुग्भयमृत्युनाशं दातारमीशं विविधौषधीनाम्॥५॥

क्षीरसागरसम्भूतं समुद्रमथनोद्भवम्।
अमृतायुर्वेददेवं धन्वन्तरिं नमाम्यहम्॥६॥

वैद्यनाथं जगन्नाथं लोकनाथमनामयम्।
सर्वरोगप्रशमनं धन्वन्तरिं नमाम्यहम्॥७॥

यो वैद्यः सकलव्याधिहारी भेषजसम्पदा।
अमृतं चामरत्वं च ददाति भुवनेश्वरः॥८॥

चतुर्दश्यां कार्तिकस्य कृष्णपक्षे समुत्थितम्।
सागरान्मथ्यमानाद्वै धन्वन्तरिमुपास्महे॥९॥

सर्वव्याधिविनाशाय सर्वमृत्युनिवारणे।
धन्वन्तरिमहाविष्णो प्रसीद परमेश्वर॥१०॥

॥ इति श्री धन्वन्तरि मन्त्र सम्पूर्णः ॥
transliteration:
|| Shrī Dhanvantari Mantra ||

|| Dhyānam ||
Śaṅkhaṁ chakraṁ jalaukāṁ cha dadadamṛtabhājanam.
Chaturbhujaṁ suprasannaṁ dhyāyeddhanvantariṁ Harim.

Nīlotpalaadalaśyāmaṁ padmālaṅkṛtabhūṣaṇam.
Chaturbhujaṁ Hariṁ vande Dhanvantarimanāmayam.

|| Dhanvantari Mūla Mantra ||
Om Namo Bhagavate Vāsudevāya Dhanvantaraye.
Amṛtakalaśahastāya sarvāmayavināśanāya
Trailokyānāthāya Śrīmahāviṣṇave Namaḥ.||1||

|| Dhanvantari Gāyatrī ||
Om Dhanvantaraye Vidmahe Divodāsāya Dhīmahi.
Tanno Viṣṇuḥ Prachodayāt.||2||

|| Dhanvantari Bīja Mantra ||
Om Hrīṁ Dhanvantaraye Namaḥ.||3||

|| Ārogya Mantra ||
Om Aiṁ Hrīṁ Śrīṁ Klīṁ Dhanvantaraye Namaḥ.
Sarvaroganivāraṇāya sarvavyādhivināśanāya
Mahāviṣṇusvarūpāya Dhanvantaraye namo namaḥ.||4||

|| Roganāśana Stuti ||
Namāmi Dhanvantarimādiadevaṁ surāsurairvanditapādapadmam.
Loke jarāRugbhayamṛtyunāśaṁ dātāramīśaṁ vividhauṣadhīnām.||5||

Kṣīrasāgarasambhūtaṁ samudramathanodbhavam.
Amṛtāyurvedadevaṁ Dhanvantariṁ namāmyaham.||6||

Vaidyanāthaṁ jagannāthaṁ lokanāthamanāmayam.
Sarvarogapraśamanaṁ Dhanvantariṁ namāmyaham.||7||

Yo Vaidyaḥ sakalavyādhihārī bheṣajassampadā.
Amṛtaṁ chāmaratvaṁ cha dadāti bhuvaneśvaraḥ.||8||

Chaturdasshyāṁ Kārtikasya kṛṣṇapakṣe samutthitam.
Sāgarānmathyamānādvai Dhanvantarimupāsmahe.||9||

Sarvavyādhivināśāya sarvamṛtyunivāraṇe.
Dhanvantarimahāviṣṇo prasīda parameśvara.||10||

|| Iti Shrī Dhanvantari Mantra sampūrṇaḥ ||
hindiMeaning: ध्यान: शंख, चक्र, जलौका (जोंक) और अमृत कलश धारण करने वाले, चतुर्भुज, अति प्रसन्न धन्वन्तरि हरि का ध्यान करे। नीलकमल दल जैसे श्यामवर्ण, पद्म-भूषण अलंकृत, रोगरहित हरि धन्वन्तरि को वन्दन। (1) मूल मन्त्र: भगवान वासुदेव धन्वन्तरि को नमस्कार, अमृत कलश हाथ में लिए, सभी रोगों का विनाश करने वाले, त्रैलोक्यनाथ श्री महाविष्णु को नमस्कार। (5) देवासुरों द्वारा वन्दित चरण-कमल, जरा-रोग-भय-मृत्यु का नाश करने वाले, विविध औषधियों के दाता आदिदेव धन्वन्तरि को प्रणाम। (6) क्षीरसागर से, समुद्र मन्थन से उत्पन्न, अमृत-आयुर्वेद देव धन्वन्तरि को नमस्कार। (9) कार्तिक कृष्ण चतुर्दशी को समुद्र मन्थन से प्रकट हुए धन्वन्तरि की उपासना करते हैं। (10) सभी व्याधियों के विनाश, सभी मृत्यु के निवारण के लिए धन्वन्तरि महाविष्णु प्रसन्न हों।
benefits: Traditionally recited for removal of all diseases through the divine physician dhanvantari
Contains Mula Mantra, Gayatri, and Bija Mantra for complete health worship
Invokes longevity and freedom from old age, disease, and fear of death
Invokes the Ayurvedic deity for holistic healing of body and mind
Especially powerful when recited on Dhanteras (Kartik Krishna Chaturdashi)
Invokes the blessing of the nectar of immortality (amrit) and divine medicine
description: Dhanvantari Mantra — धन्वन्तरि मन्त्र — is a sacred prayer offered to Lord Dhanvantari. This sacred composition is sourced from Bhagavata Purana / Ayurvedic Tradition and continues to inspire devotees worldwide. Devotees recite this prayer to invoke the Ayurvedic deity for holistic healing of body and mind and contain Mula Mantra, Gayatri, and Bija Mantra for complete health worship. It is also said to grant longevity and freedom from old age, disease, and fear of death. It is especially recommended on Saturday (Shanivar) and during Dhanteras for maximum spiritual benefit. Comprising 10 verses, it can be completed in approximately 7 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Dhanvantari Mantra in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhanvantari Mantra and what mantras does it contain?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Mantra (धन्वन्तरि मन्त्र) collection assembles the principal mantras for upasana of Lord Dhanvantari — the divine physician and Adi-deva of Ayurveda — into one comprehensive sadhana text. It contains: (1) the Dhyana shlokas describing his four-armed form bearing shankha-chakra-jalauka-amrita-ghata; (2) the Mula Mantra — Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Dhanvantaraye Amrita-kalasha-hastaya Sarvamaya-vinashanaya Trailokya-nathaya Sri Maha-Vishnave Namah; (3) the Dhanvantari Gayatri — Om Dhanvantaraye Vidmahe Divodasaya Dhimahi, tanno Vishnuh Prachodayat; (4) the Bija Mantra — Om Hrim Dhanvantaraye Namah; (5) an extended Arogya Mantra invoking the bijas Aim-Hrim-Shrim-Klim before Dhanvantari's name; and (6) closing roga-nashana stuti shlokas. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 8.8; Ayurvedic mantra tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhanvantari Mula Mantra and what does it mean?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Mula Mantra is — Om Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya Dhanvantaraye Amrita-kalasha-hastaya Sarvamaya-vinashanaya Trailokya-nathaya Sri Maha-Vishnave Namah. Word-meaning: Om — the pranava; Namo Bhagavate Vasudevaya — salutations to the Divine Lord Vasudeva (the supreme Vishnu); Dhanvantaraye — to Dhanvantari; Amrita-kalasha-hastaya — to the one bearing the pot of immortal nectar in his hand; Sarvamaya-vinashanaya — to the destroyer of all diseases (sarva-amaya); Trailokya-nathaya — to the lord of the three worlds; Sri Maha-Vishnave Namah — salutations to the great Vishnu. This twenty-syllable mantra reveals Dhanvantari as none other than Maha-Vishnu manifest as the universal physician, with the amrita-kalasha as his cosmic medicine. (Sources: Vishnu mantra-shastra; Ayurveda tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhanvantari Gayatri and how is it used?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Gayatri is — Om Dhanvantaraye Vidmahe Divodasaya Dhimahi, tanno Vishnuh Prachodayat. Word-meaning: Om Dhanvantaraye Vidmahe — 'we know (meditate upon) Dhanvantari'; Divodasaya Dhimahi — 'we contemplate the King Divodasa (Dhanvantari's incarnation as King Divodasa of Kashi who taught Ayurveda to Sushruta and other rishis)'; tanno Vishnuh Prachodayat — 'may that Vishnu illumine us.' This Gayatri uniquely identifies Dhanvantari with both the cosmic Vishnu and the historical Divodasa-Kashi-raja of the Sushruta Samhita lineage. It is recited 108 times daily by Ayurveda physicians, students at Ayurvedic colleges, and patients seeking arogya. The 108-japa is considered especially powerful on Dhanteras and during chronic illness. (Sources: Sushruta Samhita Sutrasthana; Deva-Gayatri tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dhanvantari Bija Mantra and the Arogya Mantra?
seoFaqs.answer: The compact Dhanvantari Bija Mantra is — Om Hrim Dhanvantaraye Namah — a powerful seven-syllable mantra ideal for malajapa (rosary recitation) and continuous mental japa during work, travel or illness. The Hrim bija is the maya-bija invoking the cosmic shakti of healing. The extended Arogya Mantra is — Om Aim Hrim Shrim Klim Dhanvantaraye Namah, sarva-roga-nivaranaya sarva-vyadhi-vinashanaya Maha-Vishnu-svarupaya Dhanvantaraye namo namah. The four bijas Aim (Saraswati — knowledge of treatment), Hrim (Maya — universal healing shakti), Shrim (Lakshmi — vitality and prosperity in health) and Klim (Kama — the will to live) combine to invoke complete healing. This Arogya Mantra is recited especially for chronic, incurable and life-threatening conditions when standard medicine has reached its limits. (Sources: Tantric mantra-shastra; Dhanvantari upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Dhanvantari Mantras be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dhanvantari Mantras are most powerfully recited on Dhanteras (Kartik Krishna Trayodashi — when Dhanvantari emerged from Samudra Manthana, the supreme arogya-tithi); on every Saturday (Shanivar — to neutralise Shani-induced chronic illness); on Tuesdays for blood and surgical conditions; on every Pradosh; throughout the month of Kartik; on Akshaya Tritiya; and continuously during epidemics, surgery, chemotherapy, organ failure and incurable disease. The recommended sankhya is 108 japa daily for arogya, 1008 for serious illness, and a 40-day purashcharana of 1,25,000 mantras for incurable conditions. The recitation should be done after bath at Brahma muhurta, facing east, on a tulsi or rudraksha mala, with a ghee lamp lit before a Dhanvantari image, with sankalpa for the patient by name. (Sources: Mantra-purashcharana shastra; Dhanvantari upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Dhanvantari Mantras?
seoFaqs.answer: Traditional Ayurveda acharyas and Vaishnava upasakas attribute extensive benefits to sincere Dhanvantari mantra-japa: cure of chronic and incurable diseases when standard treatment has failed; protection from epidemics, contagion and untimely death (akala-mrityu); enhancement of the efficacy of all medicines, herbs and treatments being administered; longevity (dirgha-ayushya) and the slowing of jara (ageing); protection from rogas affecting all seven dhatus and all three doshas; balancing of vata-pitta-kapha; success for Ayurveda physicians, vaidyas and surgeons in their professional work; protection of unborn children and recovery from gynaecological conditions; the merit of the amrita itself when the Mula Mantra naming amrita-kalasha-hastaya is chanted with bhava; and ultimately the cure of bhava-roga (samsara) — for Dhanvantari is none other than Maha-Vishnu. (Sources: Dhanvantari mantra phalashruti; Ayurveda tradition.)
